title: docs-sources.yaml
description: Declares where a project's documentation lives. Written as YAML at the project root. Meant to be committed and shared — keep machine-specific paths out of it. Bundled identically with the doc-staleness and pr-doc-check skills; keep both copies in sync.

ignore.description: Docs to skip, e.g. because they describe planned behavior. Each entry is a doc reference (see docRef), prefixed with a source name ("user-docs:docs/pricing.md") when the same path exists in more than one source.

code.description: Hints mapping a doc to the code it covers; used for triage and verification when inference from the doc's content would guess wrong.

paths.description: Files or directories in the code repo that this doc describes, relative to the project root.

sourceName.description: Optional label for this source; must be unique across sources. Needed only to disambiguate doc references in "ignore" and "code" when the same doc path exists in more than one source.

docRef.description: Doc path relative to its source repo's root (for "path" sources, the project root), independent of the source's "path" field. Optionally prefixed with a source name and a colon ("dev-docs:docs/auth.md") to disambiguate.

relativePath.description: Relative path: no absolute prefix, "~", drive letter, or ".." segments — the config is committed and shared, so it must not encode machine-specific locations.

path.description: Directory or file, relative to the project root (e.g. "docs/"). Docs on this machine outside the project root must be declared as a "git" or "external" source instead.

remote.description: Remote URL, ssh or https only: "git@host:org/repo.git", "ssh://host/org/repo", or "https://host/org/repo". Other transports (ext::, file://, git://) are rejected — a shared config must not be able to trigger command execution or local file access when cloned.

path.description: Optional subdirectory within that repo to limit the search to (e.g. "engineering/").

externalSource.description: Docs in an external system such as Notion or Confluence, accessed via whatever integration is available.

root.description: Page, space, or folder to start enumerating docs from (e.g. "Engineering Docs").
